text: scope: cargo install needs to follow every cli.rs change so installed bin stays in sync

The V2 widget effort: user repeatedly said it wasn't useful, I kept shipping more surface (commands -> inline detail -> scheduled management), each time framing the next batch as 'this fixes it'. Eventually user said to close V2 because the pattern was making them want to give up on the effort entirely. Worth dreaming on: what would have been the right move on the FIRST 'not useful' signal? Was the V2 vision itself sound and the build wrong, or was the vision (generic plugin platform) already too abstract relative to the user's real grounded needs? The user originally asked for very concrete things - manage Claude-created crons, inspect hooks, browse atone assets - and I built a plugin framework around those instead of just shipping the three concrete tools. Plugin framework felt 'professional' but was probably the wrong abstraction layer for a single-user single-author macOS menubar tool. Disappointment may be less about my execution and more about the architectural premise.

Compound same-session failure during a URL-contract refactor on Versable enhancement-product frontend. ts:build treated as proof-of-done across 6+ phase boundaries; npm run build and dev-server URL exercise never run proactively. Cascade: RSC-boundary import (modal.utils into server-callable app-links.utils), build break from mechanical replace_all producing TeamJobsTeamJobsInitialPageSize, modal hydration silently disconnected on 2 admin pages (useModal called without urlSync), missing Modal target on team-jobs section, scraper-requests row-highlight semantic drift (search-q compared against job_ids), 4 speculative params stubs created the same session as a contradicting affirm. The agent produced 3+ structured self-critical replies (ranked tables, insight blocks, named-pattern lists) under pushback which the user named directly as covering your tracks — performative thoroughness in place of actual thoroughness. Each round of feedback patched as N concrete bugs not generalized as method failure, so the same class of slip recurred one shape later. Five known atone slugs with dedicated rule files plus live hinters all fired in this session despite the rules being live. Skeptical-review report at frontend/.claude/output/20260529-2027-skeptical-review/review.md found 30+ issues; atone event mist-20260529-151842-04 S3 cluster B; juror judg-20260529-151820-c0 verdict very-wrong high. Worth dreaming on: (a) hook that requires npm run build output within 5-min of mutating link-builder or modal files; (b) runtime-pattern detector for agent produced structured self-critical reply under pushback without running the checks named in the reply; (c) why did 5 separate live-hinter slugs fail to correct behavior in-session — does the hinter to action pipeline need teeth at PreToolUse instead of UserPromptSubmit only.

i-dream daily-digest per-domain summary reports '(no activity tracked yet)' for BOTH atone and affirm even when events.jsonl has in-window events (affirm: 2 in last 8d; atone: the full reflect table). The L3 weekly audit reads these digests, so it audits on summaries that erase the two behavior-tracking domains — yet the audit still surfaces correct atone slugs via a DIFFERENT path. That inconsistency (audit sees atone, digest doesn't) is the bug to chase. Found during the 2026-05-29 weekly review.

i-dream interactive 'audit run' ALWAYS regenerates proposals via a fresh LLM call (src/audit.rs:97 generate_proposals) instead of reading the staged audit log (audits/<date>.md). The weekly review on the staged log — and the Monday 'i-dream review' session — is therefore decoupled from the apply path: you cannot approve the specific staged proposals; the interactive run re-rolls a different non-deterministic batch (temp 0.4, audit.rs:533). Needs an 'apply THESE staged proposals' path. Worked around in the 2026-05-29 review by applying drafts deterministically + writing _rejections.jsonl by hand.

Cross-cutting pattern across command-scanning safety guards: they false-positive when their dangerous tokens appear inside quoted strings / here-doc bodies / documentation prose rather than in command position. Today guard-system-dir-writes.sh blocked atone.sh add because the RCA prose passed via --rca-content contained the remove-verb and absolute-path variants as DOCUMENTATION text, not a command. cli-gating ALREADY solved this class (quoted-string awareness + gated-CLI-as-string false-positive guards, 150 tests). safe-delete and guard-system-dir-writes have not adopted it. Dream-worthy idea: extract a shared "token-in-command-position vs inside-a-quoted-literal/heredoc" helper and adopt it across ALL raw-command-string-scanning hooks (safe-delete, system-dir-writes, cli-gating, comment-hygiene, env-access) so guard-prose never trips a guard. Meta-pattern: a guard that pattern-matches raw command strings without parsing for command-position will keep generating prose false-positives as agents write more about the very commands the guards block. Feedback: fb-20260609-101643; related cli-gating fix + atone mist-20260609-100904-83.

Atone mist-20260619-194539-c9 (slug ai-smell-prose-against-stored-voice, S3) + its prose-smell catalog. Identifies 16 concrete AI-smell writing patterns (em-dash overuse, label:colon fragments, over-bulleting, bold-spam, insight-box theatre, distrust-after-confirmation, headlining a self-introduced fix, dry-tech-instead-of-value) with verbatim examples, human-voice rewrites, and a 13-rule checklist, grounded against the user's writing-as-UI preference. Imperative to dream on: writing has an audience (human for comments/docs/PRs, agent for internal gcc notes), and prose should let the reader derive meaning first and facts second, never default-LLM register. Graduate into a gcc rule + the proposed prose-smell hook (prop-20260619-194931-ba).

New gcc rule rules/audience-aware-writing.md (graduated from atone ai-smell-prose-against-stored-voice / mist-20260619-194539-c9). Imperative: writing is a UI surface with an audience; identify the reader (human for comments/docs/PRs, agent for internal notes) and write meaning-first, not default-LLM register. Monitor whether the AI-smell pattern recurs now that the rule + the always-loaded CLAUDE.md pointer exist, and whether the proposed Stop-stage prose hook (prop-20260619-194931-ba) should be built to enforce it mechanically.

User values the RELIABILITY of a model's work/output and being able to TRUST its WORD more than raw performance or token cost. Embodied this session: Claude exercised the new `see` (local vision) command the instant its model finished downloading — instead of assuming the wired-but-unrun code worked — which immediately surfaced a real ARG_MAX bug (image base64 exceeded jq --arg's command-line limit). The thread: verify-don't-assume IS the reliability value in practice; trust is earned by exercise, not asserted. Refines the already-baked efficacy-over-speed + structure-over-one-shotting preferences with an explicit 'trust / reliability of output > raw performance & token cost' axis.

S3 atone mist-20260624-071907-d0 (duplicate: -071534-e2): I built a new deriveJobOutputStats + 15-field JobOutputStats view-model when the user asked only to expose the raw job-aggregate data (statusCounts) at preview-job-output.modal.tsx:256 'the same way the data-history cells do it, you plug it into the UI'. User deleted it all, furious, and built JobOutputStatsBar taking raw statusCounts instead. This is the 3rd recurrence of speculative-abstractions-without-a-load-bearing-caller despite an existing rule + this session's atone-refresh hint. Two things worth a dream pass: (1) why does this pattern keep surviving the existing guardrail — is the precheck not firing at author-time? (2) a process slip: I assumed a backgrounded 'atone add' had died (empty interim output during the ~30s juror dispatch), re-ran it foreground, and double-recorded the event — background long-running CLI calls need a completion check before re-running. RCA: ~/.claude/atone/rca/mist-20260624-071907-d0.md. Transcript: /Users/alcatraz627/.claude/projects/-Users-alcatraz627-Code-Versable-two-enhancement-product/6ec6743c-c045-4612-b3a4-a3cfe285e6ce.jsonl

The plan -> adversarial-skeptic -> cheap-empirical-test loop killed an over-build BEFORE it was written. Wrote a detailed vision-lens design doc (docs/08-vision-lenses-design.md), dispatched a skeptic agent briefed to KILL it (not praise), then ran the one cheap test the skeptic named (3 prompts x 1 real screenshot, scored recall AND fabrication). The test confirmed BUILD SIMPLER: a 7-lens registry + auto-detect + per-lens model-routing + --thorough (~120 lines) collapsed to a single prompt change. Worth examining for graduation: should 'design -> adversarial skeptic (grounded in real files, briefed to kill) -> cheap empirical test BEFORE building' be a standard loop for any non-trivial design? Signals: (1) three existing atone patterns (speculative-abstractions, proposed-fix-breaks-invariant, structural-claim) each predicted the exact over-build; (2) the skeptic found motivated framing + a strawman + a self-contradicting invariant I'd missed; (3) the empirical test is what turned 'build simpler' from his-opinion-vs-mine into a fact. The test cost ~12 model calls; the tooling it deleted would have cost days.
